chapter_title.ar: باب مَا يَجِبُ عَلَيْهِ الصَّدَقَةُ مِنَ الْحَيَوَانِ وَ مَا لَا يَجِبُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 17 – What Obligates the charity (Zakāt) upon the animals, and what does not Obligate

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (16/57)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (16/57)
text: الرَّجُلِ يَكُونُ لَهُ إِبِلٌ أَوْ بَقَرٌ أَوْ غَنَمٌ أَوْ مَتَاعٌ فَيَحُولُ عَلَيْهَا الْحَوْلُ فَيَمُوتُ الْإِبِلُ وَ الْبَقَرُ وَ الْغَنَمُ وَ يَحْتَرِقُ الْمَتَاعُ قَالَ لَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ شَيْ‏ءٌ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from one of our companions,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> regarding the man who happens to have a camel, or a cow, or a sheep, or chattels. So the year passes by over it and the camel, and the cow, and the sheep dies, and the chattels burn down. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘There is nothing upon him’.
en.sarwar: 6. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from certain persons of our people who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a man who has a camel or cow or sheep or other assets. One year passes and the camel, cow or sheep dies and the asset is destroyed because of fire. He (the Imam) said, ‘He does not owe anything.’”
